**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Tianjin Yazaki Automotive Parts Co., Ltd. is a deeply rooted, fully foreign-owned (Japanese) enterprise established in 1988 within the Tianjin Binhai New Area. It functions as a critical Chinese subsidiary of the global Yazaki Corporation (Yazaki), a dominant Tier-1 supplier of automotive wire harnesses and instruments. Despite a significant paid-in capital of USD 19.63 million and a highly complex business scope encompassing auto parts, wire/cable manufacturing, battery components, and real estate leasing, a glaring anomaly is observed: recovered data for 2024 reveals only 22 insured employees. For a wire harness manufacturer—an industry notoriously characterized by its labor-intensive nature—this employee count is demonstrably insufficient for active mass production. This strongly indicates that this specific Tianjin facility has likely ceased its primary manufacturing operations, instead pivoting to function as a regional management shell, an R&D/testing node, or a landlord entity capitalizing on its substantial legacy factory assets by leasing them to other supply chain partners.
